hypotheses.root_cause: DMA RX buffers are in cacheable memory and are consumed without cache-line aligned invalidation after DMA completion.
evidence_for: serial.log reports corruption only when D-cache is enabled
dma_snapshot.yaml shows RX buffer address 0x24001204, which is not 32-byte aligned
The invalidate-on-RX experiment removes the corrupted payloads
evidence_against: No bus fault or hard fault appears in the packet
The last known good build differs only by cache behavior
hypotheses.verification_step: Move RX buffers to a non-cacheable region or align to 32 bytes and invalidate the full cache-line span after DMA completion, then run the RX stress loop.
hypotheses.expected_observation: The CRC mismatch counter remains at zero with D-cache enabled.
hypotheses.fix_if_confirmed: Align DMA buffers and descriptors to cache-line size, invalidate RX spans after DMA completion, and clean TX spans before DMA ownership transfer.

hypotheses.root_cause: The peripheral is writing outside the intended DMA buffer because of a descriptor length error.
evidence_for: DMA corruption can also come from descriptor length mistakes
evidence_against: The same descriptor configuration passes when D-cache is disabled
The cache maintenance experiment changes the outcome
hypotheses.verification_step: Dump descriptor base, length, ownership, and next pointers before and after RX completion.
hypotheses.expected_observation: A descriptor bug would show an invalid length or pointer independent of cache maintenance.
hypotheses.fix_if_confirmed: Correct descriptor sizing and ownership sequencing.
